# ViewSpec

> ViewSpec is an agent-native UI compiler: agents emit IntentBundle JSON, and ViewSpec compiles HTML, React, SwiftUI, Flutter, and CompositionIR.

ViewSpec lets agents describe semantic UI intent instead of hand-authoring DOM. The local SDK validates `viewspec.intent.json`, compiles it with `DESIGN.md`, writes `provenance_manifest.json`, and checks artifacts without SDK network calls for agentic engineering workflows.

Raw HTML tools remain available only for importing existing HTML: `viewspec compile`, `viewspec lift`, `viewspec diff`, and `viewspec check` can sanitize, theme, manifest, and diff imported HTML.

## Canonical Links

- Product and playground: https://viewspec.dev/
- Agent integration docs: https://github.com/nxrobins/viewspec/blob/main/docs/agent-integration.md
- Local HTML import docs: https://github.com/nxrobins/viewspec/blob/main/docs/local-html-wedge.md
- Cross-platform launch demo: https://viewspec.dev/cross-platform-dashboard/
- Aesthetic profiles demo: https://viewspec.dev/aesthetic-profiles/
- Custom motif demo: https://viewspec.dev/custom-motifs/
- Interactive compose demo: https://viewspec.dev/interactive-compose/
- Hosted compiler API: https://api.viewspec.dev/v1/compile
- OpenAPI description: https://viewspec.dev/openapi.json
- Agent asset manifest: https://viewspec.dev/agent-assets.json
- Agent system prompt: https://viewspec.dev/agent-system-prompt.txt
- Agent IntentBundle schema: https://viewspec.dev/agent-intent-bundle.schema.json
- Valid starter IntentBundle example: https://viewspec.dev/agent-intent-example.dashboard.json
- Python package: https://pypi.org/project/viewspec/
- Source repository: https://github.com/nxrobins/viewspec
- Full AI context: https://viewspec.dev/llms-full.txt

## Current Product Facts

- Free SDK: `pip install viewspec`
- First proof: `viewspec prove --out .viewspec-proof` writes `.viewspec-proof/PROOF.md` for humans, `.viewspec-proof/proof_report.json` for tools, and `.viewspec-proof/support_bundle.json` for redacted local support triage; `docs/proof-bundle.md` and https://viewspec.dev/proof-bundle/ explain status, hashes, checks, failure codes, and support bundles.
- Runtime LLM calls: zero in local validate/compile/lift/diff/check and zero in the live compiler path.
- Agents should emit `viewspec.intent.json` for new UI, optionally scaffold with `viewspec init-intent`, then run `viewspec validate-intent`, `viewspec diff-intent` for revisions, `viewspec compile`, and `viewspec check`.
- Use `viewspec init-design --out DESIGN.md` when the repo does not already have a design file.
- `viewspec validate-intent` exits 0 for valid intent, 2 for user-correctable invalid intent, and 1 for environment/internal failure.
- Validation issues include severity, code, path, message, suggestion, and a bounded repair_checklist; correction prompts require full IntentBundle regeneration.
- V1 local IntentBundle caps: max 256KB JSON, 200 substrate nodes, 32 regions, 400 bindings, 64 groups, 32 motifs, 400 styles, 64 actions, 64 attrs/slots/edges per node, 200 values per slot or edge, and 64 payload bindings per action.
- V1 public SDK agent ids and object keys use only letters, digits, underscore, dot, and dash.
- Native agent setup: `viewspec init-agent --target codex|claude-code|cursor|copilot|all`.
- Local agent contract asset export: `viewspec export-agent-assets --out .viewspec`.
- Local agent contract asset verification: `viewspec check-agent-assets .viewspec --json`.
- Optional MCP server: install `viewspec[agents]`, then run `viewspec mcp`.
- MCP intent tools: `init_intent`, `validate_intent_bundle_file`, `diff_intent_bundle_files`, `compile_intent_bundle_file`, `agent_correction_prompt_file`, `check_artifact`, `verify_host`, `prove`, `check_agent_assets`, `init_design`, and `export_agent_assets`.
- Raw HTML tools are import/fallback only for existing HTML.
- Generated outputs such as `dist/index.html` and `react-output/ViewSpecView.tsx` are artifacts; edit `viewspec.intent.json` or `DESIGN.md` instead.
- Agents should output IntentBundle JSON, not CompositionIR, HTML, CSS, React, SwiftUI, or Flutter source for new UI.
- Agents must not call remote reference libraries by default. If the user explicitly asks for research or repo instructions configure an approved source, references may inform semantic intent only.
- V1 public SDK motifs: table, dashboard, outline, comparison, list, form, detail, empty_state, loading_state, error_state, hero.
- V1 public SDK action kinds: select, submit, navigate, search, filter, sort, paginate, bulk_action.
- Collection actions target table/list motifs and dispatch events only; host apps own filtering, sorting, pagination, and selection state.
- V1 public SDK action target_ref is null/empty or region:id, binding:id, motif:id, or view:id.
- V1 public SDK agent binding cardinality: exactly_once.
- V1 public SDK agent group kind: ordered.
- V1 public SDK agent region layouts: stack, grid, cluster.
- V1 public SDK agent regions must form one acyclic tree rooted at view_spec.root_region.
- V1 public SDK semantic edges must reference declared substrate.nodes ids.
- V1 public SDK style targets must be explicit refs or unambiguous bare ids.
- V1 public SDK complexity_tier starts at 1, region child bounds are non-negative, and max_children is null or at least min_children.
- V1 public SDK agent style tokens must come from agent-intent-bundle.schema.json, not arbitrary CSS.
- V1 aesthetic profile tokens are deterministic art-direction handles, not CSS: aesthetic.calm_ops, aesthetic.premium_saas, aesthetic.data_dense, aesthetic.editorial_product, and aesthetic.executive_review. At most one may target view:{view_spec.id}; profiles may derive governed style projection and bounded layout metadata, and are not pixel-perfect visual proof, accessibility certification, arbitrary host-app compatibility, or design-review approval.
- The local schema and validate-intent reject hosted-only fields: root design, root motif_library, view_spec.inputs, view_spec.projections, and view_spec.rules.
- Unknown local V1 extension fields fail with UNKNOWN_FIELD instead of being ignored.
- Hosted launch emitters: HTML/Tailwind, React TSX, SwiftUI, Flutter.
- Hosted extended demo artifacts declare `contract_profile: "hosted_extended_v1"` when they include inputs, rules, projections, or mobile emitters beyond the local V1 `validate-intent` contract.
- Free hosted tier: 500 calls/day.
- Pro tier: $149/month, 10,000 hosted compile calls/day, mobile emitters, and 5 custom motif instances per compile.
- Enterprise tier: custom volume, unlimited custom motifs, org sharing, and launch support.
- Public facts manifest: https://viewspec.dev/public-facts.json
- Proof scope: ViewSpec prove is not pixel-perfect visual regression, accessibility certification, arbitrary host-app certification, or hosted compiler publish automation.

## Short Description

ViewSpec is an agent-native UI compiler: agents describe UI intent once as an IntentBundle, and ViewSpec compiles deterministic, themed, auditable UI artifacts without asking agents to author DOM.
